mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns following rate cuts over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions upward, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion months over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during periods of constrained capacity utilization signals supply-side tightening that compresses profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or flat when industrial production accelerates above 1% monthly, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0) in high-utilization periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of positive payroll months, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over any consecutive 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation exceeds 3.5% annually while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting equity market repricing toward growth assets and delivering positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of triggered months, or the inflation-rate spread fails to predict returns (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates weakening consumer spending relative to aggregate output, which forecasts earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence metric shows no statistical relationship with 20-day returns, or positive returns occur in > 60% of divergence episodes over a 3-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary expansion, rising industrial production signals capacity constraints that force firms to delay capital expenditures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rising production months during expansion regimes, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance (Rank IC < 0) in a 2-year rolling test.
